title: GPT explained visually..
summary: Caleb Writes Code provides a visual walkthrough of generative pretrained transformer architecture and mechanics. The video covers self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections as core building blocks of GPT-based models including Gemini, Opus, Kimi, Nemotron, and Qwen.
writeup: Caleb Writes Code walks through the architecture of generative pretrained transformers using visual explanations. The video breaks down the core building blocks: self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. It frames these components as the shared foundation behind GPT-based models including Gemini, Opus, Kimi, Nemotron, and Qwen, with additional treatment of sparse attention and softmax.
